prompt: Construct a TrailGoods Commerce & Logistics API supporting offline-first identity, catalog, content governance, digital assets, and multi-warehouse inventory for an outdoor trips and gear marketplace operated on a single machine. The backend uses FastAPI to expose resource-oriented interface capabilities and SQLAlchemy with PostgreSQL for durable persistence, enabling Authentication/Identity APIs (register, login, password rotation, staff/student ID binding, real-name verification submission, status query, forced logout) with business rules such as passwords minimum 12 characters with one number and one symbol, session idle timeout 30 minutes, and forced logout across all sessions after 5 failed logins within 15 minutes. Real-name verification supports personal and enterprise profiles: required fields include legal name, date of birth (MM/DD/YYYY), and a government ID image upload; automated checks are limited to local format validation, file fingerprinting, and consistency rules (e.g., DOB must indicate age >= 18), with manual reviewer decision required for approval; verification expires after 12 months and returns to expired unless renewed. Product/Service Modeling APIs manage Items across three types—merchandise (SPU/SKU), service items (guided trips, rentals), and live pets—supporting categories, tags, publish/unpublish, media attachments, spec attributes, and pricing in USD; SKU inventory must never go negative and sellable quantity must account for reserved stock. RBAC and Authorization APIs support roles: Guest (read-only public catalog), Registered User (purchase intent, reviews, asset access to own uploads), Instructor (content author for trips/services), Reviewer (verification and moderation actions), and Admin (full control), with fine-grained permissions per resource operation and mandatory audit logging for all privileged actions. Content and Review APIs support 1-5 star ratings plus structured tags, report-and-appeal workflows with deadlines (report triage within 48 hours, appeal window 14 days), sensitive-word filtering using locally maintained dictionaries, and traceability rules that preserve reviewer decisions and revision history while allowing public display to hide PII and suppressed reviews. Data models must include Users, Roles, Permissions, Sessions, IdentityBindings, VerificationCases, Items (type enum: PRODUCT, SERVICE, LIVE_PET), SPUs, SKUs, PriceBooks, Warehouses, InventoryLots, InventoryMovements, InboundDocs, OutboundDocs, Stocktakes, Reservations, Assets, ShareLinks, Reviews, Reports, and Appeals. Field constraints include unique usernames, unique staff/student IDs per institution, immutable verification case IDs, SKU codes unique within an SPU, and indexes on (user_id, created_at), (item_id, status), (warehouse_id, sku_id), and (asset_hash) for deduplication. File and digital asset management must store binaries on local disk with database metadata; uploads support resumable and batch operations, deduplicate by SHA-256, and enforce limits (images up to 10 MB, videos up to 500 MB, attachments up to 50 MB; allowed MIME types restricted). Preview generation may be limited to metadata extraction and thumbnailing where feasible offline; watermarking is optional per asset with a stored policy. Share links support expiration (default 7 days), max downloads (default 20), optional password (min 8 chars), and access logs retained 180 days. Inventory and warehousing APIs must support multi-warehouse stock, inbound/outbound documents with immutable line items once posted, stocktaking with variance reasons, reorder alerts when sellable quantity falls below a configurable threshold (default 10 units), and order-linked reservation/deduction with idempotency keys to prevent double-posting; rollback must occur automatically when an order is canceled within 30 minutes of creation. Security and privacy require encryption at rest for sensitive fields (ID numbers, DOB) and masking in all responses except to Reviewer/Admin with explicit permission. The system must run fully offline in a single Docker deployment, provide structured audit logs and request correlation IDs, and meet performance targets of p95 < 300 ms for typical queries under 50 concurrent users on a single host, with nightly local backups and integrity checksums for assets and critical tables.
